summary.intent: Fix moltis issue #657 by implementing native filesystem tools (Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Glob, Grep) to replace the existing exec-based approach, which caused model quality regressions, lacked path-level security, and had poor auditability.
summary.outcome: Phase 1 MVP fully implemented and committed (db08bd64): six native AgentTool impls in crates/tools/src/fs/, wired into gateway and policy, 761 tests passing. Post-implementation audit surfaced 8 follow-up beads; moltis-ung (cwd defaulting to process cwd) was fixed and committed in the same session.

repo: AgentTool trait is defined in crates/agents/src/tool_registry.rs:12 — name/description/parameters_schema/execute
ExecTool (crates/tools/src/exec.rs:194, ~1.7K LOC) is the canonical template for new tools: ApprovalManager, SandboxRouter, NodeExecProvider, policy.rs all wired there
file_io.rs is NOT an agent-facing tool — it's an internal helper for send_image/send_document with a 20MB cap
tools.policy is name-based glob matching — adding tool names to allow/deny is free once the tool names are registered
The 'coding' policy profile at crates/tools/src/policy.rs:33 only allows ['exec','browser','memory'] — new tools must be explicitly added or they're silently blocked
Gateway tool registration happens in crates/gateway/src/server.rs:3575, adjacent to CalcTool
moltis_metrics pipeline already provides moltis_tool_executions_total and moltis_tool_execution_errors_total — no new custom counters needed
Workspace uses nightly-2025-11-30 for fmt and clippy; just lint fails on a pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build error unrelated to tools
crates/tools/src/fs/ glob/grep tools need a workspace_root set via builder or explicit absolute path — they cannot safely fall back to process cwd

code.finding: ExecTool — canonical template for new tools, wired to ApprovalManager/SandboxRouter/policy
coding profile only allows exec/browser/memory — must extend for any new tool names
pub mod fs added here to expose the new fs module
register_fs_tools called here next to CalcTool registration

workflow: Reference repo audit order matters: claw-code was catalog-only (taxonomy value), openclaw was best for edge-case semantics (adaptive paging, edit recovery, param compat), hermes was best for agent-loop concerns (re-read tracking, path policy)
Post-implementation self-audit before committing caught 15 real gaps — habit of explicit audit pass before closing a task is high value
Discovered-from beads filed with parent links keep the gap trail visible without polluting the original task
Claude-exact parameter names (file_path, old_string, new_string, etc.) from day one avoids the normalization layer openclaw paid for post-hoc
cargo fetch --locked must be verified before commit when workspace deps are added — Cargo.lock consistency is a gate per CLAUDE.md
Clippy --all-targets -D warnings on scoped crate is faster than just lint when the workspace has pre-existing unrelated failures

friction: bd dolt pull failed at session start with 'remote origin not found' — beads may be out of sync with shared store, no local fix available
just lint fails on pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build error, forcing manual scoped clippy invocation per crate
Initial 'policy is free' claim was wrong — coding profile silently blocked all new tools; required a second read of policy.rs to catch
Glob/Grep cwd defaulting to process cwd was a silent failure mode (not a build error) that only surfaced during post-implementation audit
metrics integration required reading multiple files (process.rs, moltis_metrics) to find the existing pattern before stripping custom counter code
open_items: moltis-sug: Phase 2 — sandbox bridge (FsBridge trait, tar round-trip for Edit/Write in sandbox, native Read/Glob/Grep bridge)
moltis-8o1: Phase 3 — UX polish (adaptive paging, edit recovery, re-read loop detection, must-read-before-write invariant)
moltis-6co: Phase 4 — [tools.fs] config block + allow/deny paths, binary policy, gitignore respect, hook payload docs
moltis-ehp: P1 bug — typed error taxonomy for Read (nonexistent/permission_denied/too_large/binary/symlink as structured FsErrorKind)
moltis-rtm: P1 — MCP/plugin tool name-collision policy; 'Read' from native fs will shadow or be shadowed by any MCP Filesystem server
moltis-oc4: P2 — verify Write/Edit/MultiEdit integration with checkpoint/branch-session system
moltis-tqh: P2 bug — Read has no total-byte output cap (2000 lines × 2000 chars could return 4MB per call)
moltis-a2l: P2 — no contract test for register_fs_tools through ToolRegistry::register + execute pipeline
moltis-5dz: P2 — no fs-tools Cargo feature flag in crates/cli/Cargo.toml for opt-out
moltis-tm8: P3 — no _session_key plumbing through fs tools yet (needed for phase 3 re-read tracker)
Phase 5 / explicit out-of-scope: WASM-isolated fs tools (noted on epic but no bead)
Changes not yet merged to main — worktree is clean at db08bd64 but merge to local main is pending user confirmation
